verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} or failed test directly causes creation of a downstream {target} or formal notice
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source} or event starts a formal action plan or remediation workflow in response to the issue
verbGlossary.PRECEDES: {source}identifies {target} that later appears as a punch item at closeout
verbGlossary.ESCALATES TO: {source} of insufficient severity is upgraded to a more formal notice or {target} when unresolved or serious
verbGlossary.FOREWARNS: A proactive {target} identifies a hazard condition that, if uncorrected, could result in an incident
verbGlossary.PROMPTS SCHEDULING OF: {source} causes a new {target} or recurring check to be scheduled to monitor the condition
verbGlossary.BECOMES: {target} documenting active-construction deficiency converts to a punch item if unresolved by closeout
verbGlossary.PROMPTS: {source} causes a formal {target} to be initiated to verify the extent or resolution of the issue
verbGlossary.CONTAINS: {source} is composed of individual {target} checklist lines — each item is evaluated independently for conformance
verbGlossary.GENERATES: {source} or test produces findings documented as observations, punch items, or non-conformances
verbGlossary.RESULTS IN: {source}record as its direct output or consequence
verbGlossary.CONVERTS TO: {source}{target} item is rewritten as a punch item for tracking through the closeout resolution workflow
verbGlossary.PRODUCES: A scheduled inspection instance creates the actual inspection record when executed in the field
verbGlossary.REQUIRES: {source}mandates supporting documentation such as material test reports or certifications
verbGlossary.TRACKED BY: {source} lifecycle progress is monitored through its resolution record from creation to closure
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED BY: {source} is grouped into one of the 18 validated issue categories based on keyword clustering of its name
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BY: {source} resolution is confirmed complete through a follow-up {target} or walkthrough
verbGlossary.DECOMPOSED INTO: {source}record is broken down into individual child records for granular tracking and assignment
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTED BY: {source} is formally recorded with supporting witness statements captured during investigation
verbGlossary.PRESCRIBES: {source} record's findings dictate the specific corrective actions required to prevent recurrence
verbGlossary.IMPLEMENTED AS: {source} is translated into a specific assignable task within an action plan
verbGlossary.MANDATES: A formal violation, non-conformance, or deficiency notice contractually or regulatorily requires corrective action
verbGlossary.REQUIRES RETEST VIA: {source} for failed material requires a new material test to verify the replacement or repair meets spec
verbGlossary.FORMALIZES: {source} notice elevates an informal {target} finding into a contractual obligation to correct
verbGlossary.DETECTED DURING: {source}issue is discovered through field observations identifying clashes or constructability problems
verbGlossary.SUBSET OF: {source}issues are a filtered view of the broader coordination issue set by issue type
verbGlossary.SATISFIES: {source}test report fulfills the acceptance criteria for a specific {target} checklist item
verbGlossary.REFERENCES: An action plan line item links back to {source} or {target} that triggered the task
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: Two quality/safety metrics tend to move together across projects, indicating shared underlying conditions
verbGlossary.PREDICTS: A leading metric's trend anticipates the future trajectory of a lagging metric on the same project
verbGlossary.INVERSELY PREDICTS: {source}forecasts a lower value in the other, indicating a protective relationship
verbGlossary.INVERSE OF: {source}measure the same underlying conformance from opposite directions (pass rate vs. {target} rate)
